specific_location: South Mesa Trail about .7 miles up trail to the north.
description: Hummingbird with metallic green back and head, olive green on face around the eye with no white showing. Dark eye and black patch under the eye and throat. A slightly larger reddish patch on the throat under the black patch. Sides were an olive-brown color. Breast and belly were and off-white color. Undertail coverts were not able to be seen during the observation. Tail was black - did not see any other coloring despite multiple views from different angles. Size was indeterminate - could not ascertain if it was bigger or smaller than broad-tailed's seen earlier in afternoon.Behavior: Bird would sit at the crown of a set of shrubbery for about 2-3 minutes and then take off and shoot straight up into the air, dive almost directly down towards the small runoff creek and then land in the exact same spot. While perched it twist head to each side in quick twitchy scanning motions.Call: No recognizable call or songPlumage: Breeding
